feature_name: iOS native app — investigate wrapping the existing SvelteKit PWA as a native iOS app, including configurable server URL and persistent local storage

tasks.title: Switch SvelteKit to adapter-static
tasks.description: Replace @sveltejs/adapter-auto with @sveltejs/adapter-static in web/. Install adapter-static, update svelte.config.js with fallback: 'index.html', and create web/src/routes/+layout.ts exporting prerender = true and ssr = false. Verify the existing build still works (npm run build) and all quality gates pass (lint, check, vitest).
tasks.done_when: npm run build produces static output in web/build/. npm run lint, npm run check, and npm run test all pass. No SSR-dependent behavior is broken.

tasks.title: Make API client base URL dynamic
tasks.description: In web/src/lib/api/client.ts, change the module-level const baseUrl to a getBaseUrl() function that reads runtimeApiUrl() ?? import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL ?? defaultApiUrl() on every call. Replace all ${baseUrl} references in the file with ${getBaseUrl()}. This enables the server URL to be changed at runtime without reloading the page. Add/update unit tests to verify the function reads the latest value from window.__TASKSYNC_RUNTIME_CONFIG__.apiUrl.
tasks.done_when: baseUrl is a function (getBaseUrl), all API calls use it, existing tests pass, and a new test confirms changing window.__TASKSYNC_RUNTIME_CONFIG__.apiUrl between calls results in different base URLs.

tasks.title: Create serverUrl store with validation and persistence
tasks.description: Create web/src/lib/stores/serverUrl.ts — a new store that owns the configurable server URL. localStorage key: tasksync:server-url. Store interface: get(), set(url), clear(), getEffective(), subscribe. Validation: trim whitespace, parse with new URL(), require http: or https: protocol, strip trailing slashes, console.warn on invalid input. On init and on change, write the effective URL to window.__TASKSYNC_RUNTIME_CONFIG__.apiUrl. Create comprehensive unit tests in web/src/lib/stores/serverUrl.test.ts covering: valid URL persistence, invalid URL rejection, clear/reset, default fallback, and runtime config integration.
tasks.done_when: Store exists with full validation logic. Unit tests pass covering valid URLs, invalid URLs, clear, default fallback, and runtime config writes. lint + check + test all pass.

tasks.title: Add Server settings UI section
tasks.description: Add a 'server' section to the settings menu. Update the SettingsSectionId type in settingsMenu.ts to include 'server'. Add the section entry to baseSections (adminOnly: false, placed after 'backups' or at the end). Create web/src/lib/components/settings/ServerSettings.svelte with: a text input pre-filled with the current effective URL from serverUrl store, a Save button calling serverUrl.set(), a Reset to Default link calling serverUrl.clear(), and inline validation feedback. Update the Sidebar component to render ServerSettings when the 'server' section is selected. Update settingsMenu.test.ts for the new section.
tasks.done_when: A Server section appears in settings for all users (admin and non-admin). Typing a URL and clicking Save persists it via the store. Reset clears it. Invalid URLs show inline feedback. lint + check + test all pass.

tasks.title: Add Capacitor project and configuration
tasks.description: Install Capacitor packages in web/: @capacitor/core, @capacitor/cli (dev), @capacitor/ios. Create web/capacitor.config.ts with appId 'com.tasksync.app', appName 'tasksync', webDir 'build'. Run npx cap init and npx cap add ios to generate the ios/ directory under web/. Add appropriate .gitignore entries for Xcode derived data and build artifacts while committing the base Xcode project. Verify npm run build && npx cap sync ios succeeds without errors.
tasks.done_when: Capacitor packages installed. capacitor.config.ts exists. web/ios/ Xcode project generated. npm run build && cd web && npx cap sync ios runs without errors. Quality gates (lint, check, test) still pass.

tasks.title: Add Capacitor first-launch server URL prompt
tasks.description: When the app detects it is running inside Capacitor (via Capacitor.isNativePlatform() from @capacitor/core) and no server URL has been configured, show a one-time setup prompt asking the user to enter their server URL before proceeding. Use a localStorage flag tasksync:server-url-prompted to avoid re-prompting. This can be a simple modal or inline prompt on the login/landing page. The prompt should have: a text input, a Save button, and a Skip/Use Default option. If skipped, no URL is saved and the app continues (the user can configure later in Settings).
tasks.done_when: When running in Capacitor with no server URL configured, a prompt appears on first launch. After saving or skipping, the prompt does not reappear. In browser context, the prompt never appears. lint + check + test pass.

tasks.title: Write iOS build documentation
tasks.description: Create docs/IOS.md documenting the complete build pipeline from source to device. Include: prerequisites (macOS, Xcode 15+, Node.js 20+, CocoaPods), step-by-step build instructions (npm install, npm run build, npx cap sync ios, npx cap open ios), Xcode settings (deployment target iOS 16.0+, development team, bundle identifier), simulator vs device builds, code signing notes (free personal team vs paid developer account), TestFlight distribution steps, and troubleshooting tips. Include a section on the storage persistence guarantee (why native WKWebView avoids 7-day ITP eviction). Keep it beginner-friendly.
tasks.done_when: docs/IOS.md exists with complete, beginner-friendly build instructions. A first-time iOS developer can follow it from zero to running the app on a simulator.

history.note: Moved to discovery — routing to product-manager for requirements gathering

Moved to tasks — EM broke design into 7 discrete implementation tasks
Starting T1: Switch SvelteKit to adapter-static
Starting T2: Make API client base URL dynamic
Starting T3: Create serverUrl store with validation and persistence
Starting T4: Add Server settings UI section
Starting T5: Add Capacitor project and configuration
